texts.full_text: well as electrophysiological methods to address questions relating to the neurochemical organization of pre- and post-synaptic elements involved in central respiratory control. Quantitative electron microscopic analysis will be used to determine synapse number per unit area between neurons or on specific neuronal profiles (e..g., dendritic spines) that are immunoreactive for various neurotransmitters, neuromodulators as well enzymes and other markers of neurochemical identity in central respiratory regions. However, at higher doses (60 days), there were noticeable liver cell degeneration with associated centrilobular vacuolization in addition to mild increase of serum enzymes like aspartate aminotransferase and alkaline phosphatase. Lipopolysaccharide-induced neutrophil migration exhibited elevated counts, suggesting an enhanced immune response which would be due largely or necessarily consistent for exposure via the peritoneal route. texts.full_text: nitially to determine IBZM binding within the striatum and the cerebellum and, secondly, interstriatal IBZM binding. The results were correlated with the clinical parameters of the patients and compared with the results obtained from normal controls. No difference was found in average, specific striatal IBZM binding (basal ganglia/cerebellum ratio) between patients and controls.
